name: Nevada

examFacts.notes: Administered by Pearson VUE for the Nevada Real Estate Division (NRED). The 80-question general portion and the 40-question Nevada state-law portion are scored separately, and you need 75% on each.

intro:
The Nevada real estate salesperson exam is a tougher test than most first-time candidates expect. The Nevada Real Estate Division, the state agency inside the Department of Business and Industry that licenses salespersons and brokers, contracts with Pearson VUE to deliver it as 120 scored questions split into an 80-question general portion and a 40-question Nevada state-law portion, with two and a half hours allowed for the general questions and an hour and a half for the state questions. The two portions are scored separately and you need 75 percent on each, so a strong national score won't make up for a thin grasp of Nevada law. That is why Nevada-specific preparation matters as much as the national material.

State-specific prep matters more here than almost anywhere else. Nevada tests its own rules in ways a national course never touches, from the duties a licensee owes under the agency disclosure and confirmation forms, to the consent-to-act form that lets a brokerage represent both sides of a deal, the Seller's Real Property Disclosure required on residential homes, the resale package a seller owes a buyer in a common-interest community, Nevada's prior-appropriation water rights, and the disciplinary powers of the Real Estate Division backed by the Education, Research and Recovery Fund. None of that looks like the material a candidate in a neighboring state studies. A generic real estate practice test won't cut it. You need Nevada-specific practice questions.

Below are 20 free Nevada real estate test questions and answers from RealReady's full Nevada bank. Each tests a specific topic with the correct answer and a plain-English explanation under the card. Read each one, commit to an answer, then reveal. Treat it like a real NRED practice test, and the ones you miss show you what to focus on next.

Get the RealReady app on iPhone, iPad, or Android for the rest of the Nevada question bank. The full app includes short articles that walk you through the why behind each topic, detailed explanations on every question, a missed-question drill mode, and progress tracking that shows your per-category accuracy.

faq.q: How many questions are on the Nevada real estate exam?
faq.a: The Nevada Real Estate Salesperson Examination has 120 scored multiple-choice questions, split into an 80-question general (national) portion and a 40-question Nevada state-law portion. Pearson VUE delivers both portions in one computer-based session and mixes in five to ten unscored pretest questions on each portion. The pretest questions look just like the scored ones and don't count toward your result.

faq.q: What's the passing score for the Nevada real estate exam?
faq.a: 75 percent on each portion, and the two portions are scored separately. That works out to 60 of 80 on the general portion and 30 of 40 on the Nevada portion. If you pass one portion and fail the other, you only retake the portion you failed, as long as you pass both within twelve months. Miss that window and you start over with both portions.

faq.q: How much does the Nevada real estate exam cost?
faq.a: The examination fee is $100, paid to the Nevada Real Estate Division through its online licensing portal rather than to the testing company. One detail catches people off guard. A retake costs the same as the first attempt, so even when you only need to redo one of the two portions you still pay the full $100, which makes it worth walking in prepared for both halves. If you pass, the salesperson license fee is a separate $125.

faq.q: How long is the Nevada real estate exam?
faq.a: Four hours of seat time total. Pearson VUE allows two and a half hours (150 minutes) for the 80-question general portion and an hour and a half (90 minutes) for the 40-question Nevada portion, taken in one sitting. You get your official pass or fail score report before you leave. Pearson VUE runs test centers in Las Vegas, Reno, and Elko, and also offers OnVUE online proctored testing from home.

faq.q: Is the Nevada real estate exam hard?
faq.a: It's a serious test, and the part that trips people up is the 40-question Nevada portion. The general half covers the same principles taught everywhere, but the state questions reward candidates who actually studied Nevada license law, the duties a licensee owes under the agency disclosure rules, the consent-to-act form for representing both sides, the Seller's Real Property Disclosure, and how earnest money and trust accounts have to be handled. Most people who fail leaned too hard on national material and underprepared on Nevada law. Honest reps on Nevada-specific practice questions are the difference between a first-time pass and a $100 retake.

faq.q: What's on the Nevada real estate exam?
faq.a: The 80-question general portion covers real property characteristics and legal descriptions, forms of ownership and transfer of title, property value and appraisal, contracts and agency, the practice of real estate including fair housing, property disclosures and environmental issues, financing and settlement, and real estate math. The 40-question Nevada portion covers the duties and powers of the Real Estate Commission and Division, licensing rules, the activities that require a license, agency and the duties owed by a Nevada licensee, required disclosures such as the Seller's Real Property Disclosure and common-interest community resale packages, contracts along with the handling of earnest money and advance fees, record keeping, and special topics like timeshares, water rights, and solar. License practice and contracts are the two heaviest Nevada topics, so they are worth extra attention.

faq.q: What's the best way to prepare for the Nevada real estate exam?
faq.a: After you finish Nevada's required pre-license coursework, the highest-leverage move is grinding Nevada-specific practice questions with honest feedback on every miss. National principles you can pick up from any course, but the 40-question state portion rewards repetition on the license law, agency and the duties owed by a Nevada licensee, the required property disclosures, and trust account and earnest money handling. The RealReady app gives you the full Nevada question bank plus the national bank, detailed explanations, a missed-question drill mode, and per-category progress tracking so you can see exactly where you're weak. Most users study 30 to 60 minutes a day for two to four weeks before sitting the exam.
